failedAssertions.reason: Simulator attempts to execute .DATA segment definitions as instructions - all 14 example presets contain .DATA segments causing 'Unknown instruction' errors
Blocked by P5-SIM-004 failure - cannot test continuous execution when single-step is broken
Blocked by P5-SIM-004 failure - cannot test breakpoints without working execution
Memory UI present but updates on execution could not be verified - blocked by P5-SIM-004
Cannot verify instruction highlighting - execution broken and line numbers don't match displayed code
Speed slider UI present but actual speed adjustment could not be verified - blocked by P5-SIM-004

appliedUpdates.description: Added Phase 5 Simulator flow validator guidance with 10 assertions to test

rootCause: Simulator execution engine bug: All 14 example presets contain .DATA segment definitions (e.g., 'MSG DB ...', 'TEMP DB ?', 'COUNT DB 10'), but the simulator attempts to execute these data definitions as if they were instructions. The simulator does not properly parse and skip .DATA segment definitions before executing code segment instructions. This makes it impossible to test any execution-related functionality (Step, Run, Breakpoint, Memory updates, Instruction highlighting, Speed control).
fixRecommendations: Fix simulator to properly parse and skip .DATA segment definitions when executing code
Option A: Modify the execution engine to recognize .DATA directives and skip them
Option B: Provide example presets that only contain code segments without .DATA definitions
Option C: Ensure the simulator properly handles assembly directives vs actual instructions
